title: Tempting Twice German Chicken Skewers
description: A unique German dish featuring marzipan and double-fried chicken, made Whole30-friendly by using compliant ingredients. This adventurous recipe combines the flavors of mustard and aerated textures.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the mustard, eggs, and a pinch of salt.
Add the chicken pieces to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the mustard mixture.
Thread the coated chicken pieces onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Dip each skewer into the crushed marzipan, pressing gently to adhere.
Dredge the marzipan-coated skewers in the coconut flour, shaking off excess.
Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and fry the skewers until golden brown.
Transfer the fried skewers to a baking sheet and bake in the preheated oven for 15 minutes.
Increase the oven temperature to 425 degrees Fahrenheit and bake for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the skewers from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
